instructions: (Claude Code's built-in system prompt; see --bare mode docs)

queries.user_text:
You're debugging a real-codebase bug in `yara-5` (project `yara`, an open-source C/C++ project from the BugsC++ corpus).

The buggy source tree is on the host at `C:\Users\Owner\OneDrive\Documents\Classes\COS\COS484\chatdbgpro\data\workspaces\yara-5\yara\buggy-5` and is also mounted at `/work` inside a Linux/amd64 container named `bench-t4-9f67f3772aef4f5ab60f` (gdb, the buggy binary, and all build deps live in that container; the binary is NOT runnable on this host directly).

Buggy binary inside the container: `(not pre-identified — find via failing-test command)`
Failing test invocation:           `bash -c bash -c 'echo return 238 > tests/defects4cpp.lua' && make -j1 check`
Observed behavior:                  `timeout`
Bug type:                          `other`
Language:                          `c`

How to investigate
------------------
* To READ source code, use Read / Grep / your normal tools — the source tree is at `C:\Users\Owner\OneDrive\Documents\Classes\COS\COS484\chatdbgpro\data\workspaces\yara-5\yara\buggy-5` (it's been added via `--add-dir`).
* To RUN the binary, run gdb, or execute anything else inside the build environment, use Bash with this template:

      docker exec -i -w /work bench-t4-9f67f3772aef4f5ab60f bash -c '<cmd>'

  e.g.
      docker exec -w /work bench-t4-9f67f3772aef4f5ab60f bash -c 'ls -la'
      docker exec -w /work bench-t4-9f67f3772aef4f5ab60f bash -c 'gdb -batch -ex run -ex bt --args bash -c bash -c 'echo return 238 > tests/defects4cpp.lua' && make -j1 check'

  The container is dedicated to this case; it'll be torn down when this session ends, so don't worry about cleanup.

Final answer
------------
Identify the root cause in the source, propose both a local fix and a structural global fix.

Your final response MUST include three labelled paragraphs:

  ROOT CAUSE: <file:line and what is wrong>
  LOCAL FIX:  <minimal code change>
  GLOBAL FIX: <structural change preventing this CLASS of bug>

Do NOT modify the source files on disk. Just investigate and produce the diagnosis as your final assistant message.

queries.response:
Now I have the complete picture. Let me look at the exact fix location one more time to make sure I have the right analysis:

ROOT CAUSE: In `libyara/parser.c:855-876`, the function `yr_parser_reduce_rule_declaration_phase_1` sets `compiler->current_rule_idx` **before** checking if a rule with the same identifier already exists. When the duplicate identifier check fails at line 876, the function returns an error, but `current_rule_idx` has already been set to an index that doesn't have an allocated rule. When `yyerror` is called during error handling, it tries to access the rule at that index via `yr_arena_get_ptr()` at lexer.c:3463, causing the assertion failure because the offset exceeds the allocated buffer size.

LOCAL FIX: Move the duplicate identifier check (lines 863-877) **before** the assignment at lines 855-856.

GLOBAL FIX: Add defensive bounds checking in `yyerror` (lexer.c:3463) to validate that `current_rule_idx * sizeof(YR_RULE)` doesn't exceed the allocated buffer before calling `yr_arena_get_ptr()`. This prevents similar crashes from uninitialized indices in error paths.

---

**ROOT CAUSE:** libyara/parser.c:855-876 — `compiler->current_rule_idx` is assigned to an unallocated rule index before validating that the rule identifier doesn't duplicate an existing rule. If a duplicate is found (ERROR_DUPLICATED_IDENTIFIER), the function returns early with `current_rule_idx` pointing to a rule that was never allocated. When `yyerror` (lexer.c:3463) tries to access this invalid index via `yr_arena_get_ptr()`, the offset exceeds the buffer's used size, failing the assertion at arena.c:391.

**LOCAL FIX:** In `libyara/parser.c`, move the duplicate identifier check (lines 863-877) to execute **before** lines 855-856 that set `current_rule_idx`. This ensures the index is only set if the identifier is valid.

**GLOBAL FIX:** In `libyara/lexer.c:3463` within the `yyerror` function, add a bounds check before calling `yr_arena_get_ptr()`:
```c
if (compiler->current_rule_idx != UINT32_MAX &&
    compiler->current_rule_idx * sizeof(YR_RULE) <= arena->buffers[YR_RULES_TABLE].used)
  current_rule = yr_arena_get_ptr(compiler->arena, YR_RULES_TABLE, ...);
```
This defensive check prevents crashes when error handlers encounter partially-initialized parser state from early validation failures.

tool_calls.call: timeout 10 docker exec -i -w /work bench-t4-9f67f3772aef4f5ab60f bash -c 'echo return 238 > tests/defects4cpp.lua && make -j1 check' 2>&1 | head -100
timeout 30 docker exec -i bench-t4-9f67f3772aef4f5ab60f bash -c 'echo return 238 > tests/defects4cpp.lua && make -j1 check 2>&1' 2>&1 | head -200
docker exec -i bench-t4-9f67f3772aef4f5ab60f cat test-rules.log | head -100
docker exec -i bench-t4-9f67f3772aef4f5ab60f bash -c 'echo return 238 > tests/defects4cpp.lua && gdb -batch -ex run -ex bt --args ./test-rules 2>&1' | tail -100

docker exec -i bench-t4-9f67f3772aef4f5ab60f bash -c 'cd /work && gdb -batch -ex run -ex "bt full" -ex "frame 6" -ex "p compiler->current_rule_idx" -ex "p arena->buffers[1].used" --args ./test-rules 2>&1' | tail -60
